fullName: Toby Maguire - Business and Resilience Coach
title: Founder and CEO at Living In Balance Ltd
summary:
🔷You’re successful in hospitality. 
🔷Your business runs. 
🔷Your team performs. 
🔷From the outside, everything looks solid. 
🔷But your mind rarely switches off. 
🔷You carry staff challenges home. 
🔷You wake at 2am replaying conversations. 
🔷You feel responsible for everything — and everyone. 

Hospitality is high-pressure, emotional work. And many leaders operate in a constant state of activation without real recovery. Over time, that takes a toll. 

I work with hospitality leaders who want to reduce stress, create a better work/life balance and lead with clarity — without stepping away from their careers. 

Using a structured process that combines meditation, coaching and nervous system regulation, I help clients: 

✅ Manage stress without suppressing it 
✅ Regain mental clarity and focus
✅ Improve emotional regulation under pressure 
✅ Restore energy and enthusiasm for their work 
✅ Create sustainable work-life boundaries 

With over 30 years immersed in meditation and stress management, I bring a calm, practical and results-focused approach to leadership development. This isn’t about escaping pressure. It’s about strengthening your capacity inside it.

If you’re feeling successful, but stretched, you’re welcome to book a private exploration conversation below. 

https://tobymaguire.as.me/schedule/437524d2
name: Toby Maguire - Business and Resilience Coach

summary: Over the past three decades, I have had the privilege of working across the wellness and hospitality worlds, helping shape how people experience health, travel and wellbeing. I am the Founder and CEO of GOCO Hospitality, where I lead a talented team in creating and managing wellness destinations worldwide, ranging from individual hotel spas to full-scale wellness resorts and master-planned communities. My work has taken me to 42 countries, contributing to over 400 projects with a combined value exceeding USD 4.3 billion.At GOCO, and as Managing Director of Horwath HTL Health & Wellness, I stay deeply involved in strategy, design and operations, from that first sketch on paper to opening day and beyond. I also own and manage Glen Ivy Hot Springs, the oldest and highest-grossing hot spring resort in the USA, where we continue to reinvest in its heritage and future. Through GOCO’s partnership with Fullerton Fund Management, we are actively investing in wellness-centric properties across the Asia Pacific region. We have acquired two wellness retreat assets on Koh Samui, Thailand and Bali, Indonesia, that we are now re-conceptualising and will relaunch in 2026. This collaboration reflects our shared vision to democratise access to meaningful wellness experiences and respond to the surging demand for wellness real estate.In a former life, I served on the operating board of Mandarin Oriental and led operations for Oberoi Hotels & Resorts. I was fortunate to play a role in shaping iconic destinations like Ananda in the Himalayas. Beyond my work with GOCO, I am a partner in Space Yoga and Space Cycle, and I sit on advisory boards for AMAALA (Saudi Arabia) and the International Hospitality Investment Forum. I frequently speak at international forums on wellness, hospitality and real estate, and I remain passionate about blending ancient healing wisdom with modern science to create spaces where people can feel better, live healthier and connect more deeply with themselves and the world around them.

title: Founder
summary:
Patrick Keane | Architect | Founder & Director, Enter Projects Asia

I am an architect and founder of Enter Projects Asia, an award-winning practice working across Asia, the Middle East, and Australia at the intersection of architecture, craft, large-scale infrastructure and recently luxury interiors for several of the most distinguished fashion brands globally. 

My work focuses on re-imagining contemporary architecture through plant-based and natural material systems—particularly rattan—scaled for complex environments such as airports, transport hubs, hotels, and civic spaces. By combining digital design and fabrication with traditional artisanal knowledge, our projects deliver environments that are environmentally responsive, culturally grounded, and operationally robust.

With professional experience across five continents and education spanning Australia and the United States, I bring a global perspective grounded in regional specificity. I have led multidisciplinary teams on projects ranging from intimate crafted interiors to major international infrastructure, often in challenging climatic and logistical contexts and also masterplanning sites to result in best in class outcomes. 

At Enter Projects Asia, we collaborate closely with clients, consultants, and craftspeople to develop solutions that move beyond decoration—embedding sustainability, identity, and long-term value into the architecture itself.

I am particularly interested in partnerships with forward-thinking developers, airport operators, and public institutions seeking authentic, climate-responsive design that can operate at scale.
titleDescription:
Enter Projects Asia is a leading sustainable design firm that provides Architecture and Interior services  Thailand, South East Asia, now Europe USA and Africa. Our company is fully Thai registered and operational in Thailand. Post Pandemic, we have just had a very strong upturn as wellness principles become mainstream, and our work resonates with the attitude “Going local with cutting edge International design”.  Recently we were rated in the top 30 firms of Thailand (number 12) by Architizer New York. Sustainable design blended with technology and fabrication is our area of strong focus with contemporary aesthetics and unique outcomes.

Our services include resort work, master planning , rooftop restaurants, coffee headquarters, flagship restaurants, and co-working spaces all designed to a very unique distinctive brand focused agenda, revising the norm in these design spaces. Clients include Dusit, Banyan Tree, Minor International, Frasers Property, Vikasa and a host of private operators.
 
We currently have a core team of 15 with an associated team of 45 inclusive of fabrication experts in our newly opened sustainable rattan factory North Thailand. This skillset is based in Thailand, Japan, Australia and India. We have just completed the public spaces in Terminal2  for Kempegowda airport Bangalore to be one of the largest sustainable rattan projects in existence with an airport footprint of 120,000 sqm and a project space of 12,000 sqm
 
Aside from our architecture business, we have an initiative called "Project Rattan” started in the lockdown which focuses on sustainable upgrades. Using the latest technologies and readily available materials, the initiative offers a modern pairing to our already creative design business.  “Project Rattan”  deploys local craft dovetailed with international design.

PropertyScout, a Thailand based Proptech start up, is an end-to-end real estate transaction platform connecting over 7,000 agents and agencies with buyers, renters and owners, servicing thousands of satisfied customers every month. PropertyScout collects the real estate brokerage commission from the seller or owner of a rental property and shares the commission with the agent(s) involved which can be 1 up to 3 agents. 

PropertyScout leverages its database of more than 250,000 properties, its technology platform and its centralized operations to digitize and automate 80% of the real estate transaction. Providing a transparent and trusted experience, PropertyScout has quickly become the favorite go-to-place for buying, selling, and renting real-estate in Thailand, as proven by hundreds of five-star reviews.

summary:
I’m Dr. Jetbodin Prakoonsuksapan (Fame)—a doctor-turned-entrepreneur and the Founder & CEO of Aura Wellness Group, best known for Aura Bangkok Clinic, a leading aesthetic dermatology platform in Thailand.

I founded Aura in 2016 with a simple belief: aesthetic care should be premium in quality, consistent in outcomes, and ethically delivered—without being out of reach. Since then, we’ve scaled from a single clinic into a multi-branch network across Bangkok, serving both Thai residents and international clients, while building a brand known for trust, safety, and repeat loyalty.

Today, my focus is building Aura as a high-performance, multi-site healthcare services company—where clinical excellence is supported by operational rigor. I no longer practice clinically (since 2018); instead, I lead strategy, finance, and brand, working with our medical leadership and teams to standardize care, elevate the client experience, and scale responsibly.

What I spend my time on:
	•	Multi-branch operations & unit economics: productivity, utilization, cost discipline, and margin expansion without compromising medical standards
	•	Clinical quality at scale: protocols, training systems, safety governance, and consistency across locations
	•	Brand & growth strategy: positioning, retention-led marketing, and customer experience design
	•	Systems & execution: digital infrastructure, data-driven decision making, and scalable org design
	•	Platform expansion: developing complementary wellness and spa concepts under the Aura ecosystem
	•	Product-minded tech enablement: building internal tools that connect POS, inventory, CRM, and supplier workflows into a scalable operating system for multi-site care

We’re also quietly investing in a next-generation clinic operations and commerce layer—the kind of work where product, data, and engineering sit alongside frontline healthcare to improve real-world outcomes. If you’re excited about building practical tech in a high-velocity, multi-branch environment, I’d love to connect.

My background includes a Doctor of Medicine (M.D.) and I stay deeply engaged with evidence-based dermatology—ensuring that the medicine remains authentic even as the company scales.

I’m always open to connecting with people building the future of healthcare services, consumer health, aesthetics, and scalable clinical operations—especially around partnerships, talent, and long-term platform building.

titleDescription:
THE MONDIALE DIFFERENCE

Marketing Services
Unlike many of our competitors, Mondiale offers a full suite of marketing services, all sitting on the one CRM platform. This means that at any time in the future, Mondiale can expand your membership programme to further meet the Hotel’s marketing needs or assist the Hotel with other marketing services, rather than the Hotel needing to source multiple suppliers.

Membership Package Fulfilment
Mondiale ensures that members receive their complete membership package, including an embossed (plastic) membership card as quickly as possible. Even within 15 minutes of joining if so desired.

Database Access
Mondiale encourages your Hotel management to have total access to the membership database and CRM system. This access includes full data extraction functionality. The database is yours (contractually) so we therefore facilitate your access and use of this as much as desired.

Electronic Vouchers
Mondiale has invested heavily in technology and is the only marketing company to offer electronic vouchers in our programmes (where desired). This saves enormously on time and cost in terms of package preparation. It also ensures that vouchers cannot be “lost or stolen” and that there is a 100% accurate record of what e-vouchers have been redeemed.

Tracking of Membership Usage
All components of the membership programme are tracked, from a global impact perspective across the entire hotel operation, right through to the individual member restaurant check on any given visit. Like all aspects of our technology, we happily give access to the Hotel.

I’ve spent the last 20 years working in the hotel industry across three different continents. My experience ranges from Budget all the way up to Luxury Hotels. Although 20 years is a long time for some, it still feels the same as it did on day one and I still get the same buzz from interacting with guests and colleagues.
 
My journey first began as a F&B and Kitchen Attendant, from where I moved up into different roles, gaining experience across numerous departments along the way and eventually achieving my current role of General Manager at Grand Mercure Khao Lak Bangsak. 
 
I bring a guest-centric mindfulness that allows me to find new and interesting ways of engaging and satisfying guests. I am a solid and reliable operator who can handle day-to-day challenges calmly and effectively, and have had my fair share of crises to deal with over the years. I believe my time spent working within the day-to-day operations has given me a good grounding, which allows me to better understand the challenges of all teams and departments. In recent years, I have developed a good understanding of distribution, sales and marketing and feel confident leading all areas of a hotel team.
 
I truly believe that inclusion, compassion and humility are the most effective ways to be successful within our industry, a bit of humor and fun around the hotel is also a must for me. I benefited massively from the time others put into my development and I try to pay this forward as often as possible. In my opinion, having a happy and engaged team always gets the best results.
 
When I have time off, I love to spend it outdoors and have taken up a number of water sports since moving to Thailand. A perfect day off for me is time spent with loved ones on the beach. 
 
If you are interested in speaking to me further, please feel free to reach out on direct message.

I work across several areas of the golf industry, but my focus is consistent: helping golf courses achieve high-performing surfaces through evidence-based turf management, developing future industry leaders, and driving a stronger commitment to sustainability.

As Managing Director of BRT Agronomy, I provide independent agronomy consulting to clubs across Asia. I work closely with superintendents, owners, and committees to improve turf quality, optimise maintenance programs, solve complex agronomic challenges, especially in tropical environments, and support long-term strategic planning to ensure consistent performance and sustainable growth.

At the same time, I serve as Director of Golf Course Maintenance at Nikanti Golf Club in Thailand, where our goal is simple: to deliver the best golf experience in Asia, bar none. I'm proud to lead an incredible team and help set new benchmarks for consistency, conditioning, and innovation in course management.

I also work with the GEO Foundation for Sustainable Golf as an Independent Verifier, supporting clubs and tournament organisers in their pursuit of credible, measurable sustainability. That includes reviewing GEO Certified® applications and ensuring best practices across biodiversity, water, energy, waste, and community engagement.

Whether I'm on a construction site, walking fairways, or reviewing sustainability plans, I’m always looking for practical ways to improve golf course conditioning and preserve it for the future.
titleDescription:
As Director of Golf Course Maintenance at Nikanti Golf Club, I lead all facets of turfgrass management and agronomy at one of Asia’s most innovative and highly regarded golfing destinations. My role is closely aligned with the club’s bold vision: to deliver the best golf experience in Asia.

With a predominantly Thai-speaking team and leadership group, over 90% of my daily communication is conducted in Thai, allowing me to work effectively across all levels of the operation and foster strong relationships throughout the organisation.

Key responsibilities and achievements:
- Oversee the daily maintenance and long-term conditioning of the course, ensuring premium playing surfaces across greens, tees, fairways, and surrounds.
- Drive a strategic maintenance approach focused on excellence in playability, sustainability, and visual presentation.
- Lead and mentor a skilled team of greenkeepers, building a performance-driven culture that prioritises precision, accountability, and growth.
- Implement data-informed agronomy practices around growth regulation, soil moisture management, and organic matter management, to deliver consistently high-performing surfaces while safeguarding the lifespan of the asset.
- Champion sustainability efforts aligned with GEO Foundation principles, with an emphasis on efficient resource use and environmental responsibility.
- Collaborate with senior leadership to continuously elevate the guest experience through superior course presentation and innovation.

Under my direction, Nikanti continues to set benchmarks in conditioning, consistency, and sustainable excellence, fulfilling its mission to stand apart as the leading golf experience in the region.

As CEO of Thailand Retirement Living (TRL), part of the Global Retirement Living brand, I am leading the development of premium retirement communities in Thailand designed to offer retirees a secure, comfortable, and lifestyle-focused environment centered on long-term wellbeing, safety, and quality of life.

My family has been in construction for over 100 years and in the senior-living industry for more than forty years in the UK, where we have built, owned, and operated senior living facilities to this day. With this foundation — and having personally lived and owned property-related businesses in the UK, New Zealand, Australia, Cuba, and Thailand for almost four decades — I bring a deep understanding of both international construction standards and the specific needs of retirees. My industry expertise has continued to grow over the past two decades in Thailand’s construction and property sectors.

TRL focuses on developing community driven projects with peaceful surroundings that support ease of living, independence, and meaningful community connection. Each community is crafted to provide a warm, engaging environment where residents can enjoy comfort, security, and a genuine sense of belonging.

As part of the broader Global Retirement Living brand, TRL is being positioned for rollout with well-established development frameworks and trusted local partnerships supporting long-term growth across the region.

My commitment is to bring forward retirement living solutions where residents can live comfortably, independently, and confidently — supported by thoughtful design, quality environments, and a clear long-term vision.
titleDescription:
Following on from our CEO’s families success in the retirement home industry in the UK, over the last 30-years, Palm Group Asia in partnership with Koh Samui Retirement Village (KSRV) is proud to bring the first premium retirement living resort to Koh Samui, Thailand. 

Set in four Acres of lush forest, KSRV has paved the way for a new retirement living experience offering excellence in design, lifestyle, service, security and care. KSRV offers quality independent living with the services and facilities residents would expect to find in a boutique hotel.

Downsizing your home doesn’t mean you have to downsize your lifestyle. With KSRV, residents enjoy the relaxing environment, warm community and 24-hour service. The great benefit of our concept is that you can share it all with your friends and family. Invite them to stay for dinner in the restaurant, a dip in the pool, a movie in the cinema, or to stay in your spacious and luxurious villa.

Residents have maximum choice and time to pursue an active and stimulating lifestyle, all with the comfort of knowing that if and when care is required, it is discreetly available. KSRV caters for the local community, enabling retirees to downsize in their local area and continue to access and maintain their community, family and social connections. At KSRV our Developer is our Operator, giving residents peace of mind that the company is committed to their long-term goals.

I am a French software engineer and tech entrepreneur based in Bangkok, with more than 20 years of experience building software products and development teams.

I started my career as a software engineer and project manager working on large-scale industrial and energy systems (Alstom, GDF, EDF). Since then, I have remained deeply technical (I code every day) while moving progressively into software architecture, product strategy, and company building.

In 2013, I co-founded Outsourcify, a Bangkok-based software development company that today employs around 40 engineers, designers, and QA specialists. We design and build custom digital products for startups, SMEs, and institutions worldwide.

Our work typically includes:
• product discovery and requirements workshops
• UX/UI design
•  custom web and mobile application development
•  software architecture and cloud infrastructure

We operate with fully in-house teams in Bangkok and focus on open technologies such as JavaScript, PHP, Python, Flutter, and modern cloud stacks.

Beyond the agency, I am also involved in several product initiatives and investments in the travel, hospitality and education technology space, including:
• Aroi Restaurants: a restaurant platform and reservation management system developed for the Thai market
• Axiome / The DMC Platform: travel industry infrastructure connecting European travel agencies with destination management companies worldwide
• AcadAsia: a technology-driven platform helping families navigate the international school landscape in Thailand by comparing schools, calculation tuition fees, and navigating the admissions process.

Recently I have been working extensively on how AI is transforming software development workflows, particularly through natural-language programming, AI-assisted architecture, and structured development processes that prevent the technical debt often created by uncontrolled “vibe coding”.

I occasionally speak about these topics, including lectures on AI and software development at Chulalongkorn University in Bangkok.

title: Co-Guardian
summary:
Fred Mouawad is a portfolio entrepreneur and the founder of Synergia One Group of Companies, which encompasses the various ventures he has created, including his family’s fourth-generation jewelry business, Mouawad. Synergia One has operated in over 20 countries across multiple industries, reflecting Fred's diverse business interests.

Fred overseas several companies under Synergia One, including Mouawad, the renowned high-end jewelry house that has handled some of the world’s largest and rarest diamonds. His ventures also span food manufacturing with Synova, software as a service with Taskworld, publishing with New York-based Smart Work Media (which has earned over 190 publishing awards), and CI Media, a social impact media platform company. From 1997 to 2017, Fred founded and led Global Franchise Architects, a food service company that created five brands and grew to 240 stores across seven countries.

With extensive experience in incubating and scaling ventures internationally and managing a diverse portfolio, Fred has developed comprehensive management systems that drive execution and maximize value across his companies.

Fred was raised in Geneva, Switzerland, where he attended Collège du Léman. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration from Pepperdine University and is a Graduate Gemologist from the Gemological Institute of America. He is an alumnus of the MBA Harvard Business School (1995) and of the Stanford Executive Program (SEP 2012) at the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

An active member of YPO, Fred co-founded and co-chaired the YPO Stanford GSB Program from 2014 to 2022. He has helped incubate seven YPO chapters, served as Chapter Chair for two chapters, and was the Southeast Asia Regional Chair from 2017 to 2019. In recognition of his leadership, Fred received the prestigious YPO Alexander Cappello Award in 2016.

In 2012, Fred co-founded the Harvard Business School Association of Thailand and served as its Vice President until 2022. He is also a Founding Member of Family Business eXponential (FBX), which empowers multi-generational family business success, and has been a member of the Harvard Business School Global Advisory Board since 2018.
titleDescription:
MOUAWAD – Crafting the extraordinary since 1890.  Led by fourth-generation Co-Guardians, Fred, Alain, and Pascal Mouawad, and backed by more than a century of excellence in its field, Mouawad remains a family business built upon artistic excellence, superb craftsmanship, trust, and relationships. 

In addition to its jewelry and timepiece design and production, Mouawad also manufactures rough diamonds as a Diamond Trading Company (DTC) Sightholder, a privilege that grants it a direct and unique access to conflict-free rough diamonds and gemstones, sourced and purchased in countries that are full participants in the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme. Diamonds and gemology have always been a passion for the Mouawads, and besides being renowned for fine jewelry and timepieces, the Mouawad family has acquired over the years some of the largest and rarest diamonds in the world, and many of these historic gems carry Mouawad as part of their designated name. The family’s ongoing support of the global industry through research and education led to third-generation Robert Mouawad receiving a Lifetime Achievement Award from the Gemological Institute of America and having the GIA campus named in his honor. 

Mouawad has been honored by the Guinness World Records five times, the most recent of which being for the Flower of Eternity Jewelry Coffer ― the most valuable jewelry box in the world (US $3.5M).

title: Owner
summary:
Raj Kriangsaksakul is the founder of R&R Apparel International Co. Ltd., a vertically integrated custom apparel company wholly owning the Heritage House Custom Tailors brand, as well as the completely in-house manufacturing unit comprising of almost fifty full time tailors, pattern makers and various assistive staff.

Raj brings decades of experience in the custom tailoring arena that he honed over the years being raised in the shop fronts his father owned and operated in Bangkok, Thailand all through the 1980s to early 2000s, eventually being formally inducted to the business after completing a business degree Magna Cum Laude from Bentley University.  Upon firmly deciding that he will dedicate his career to growing the legacy that his father had created up until then, he earmarked on a steady growth of the family business through the concept of ‘Come to You’ tailoring where he would meet his clients instead of them coming to him.  The concept was an instant hit with his initial set of clients, and the business has grown several times over with concrete and soon to be launched strategies that will seamlessly integrate several IT innovations into all aspects of the business - front-facing as well as in the operational and manufacturing phases; still firmly keeping intact the integrity and experience that goes into commissioning a fully custom tailored and bespoke product.

Apart from the inspiration and confidence he receives from the support of his external and internal customers; he draws a strong sense of purpose and well-being from his wife and two daughters, keeping Raj razor focused on his goals and aspirations (his daughters, however, may say that Daddy loves being a drama-King at home!).  He has also written an unpublished book dedicated to his wife, which he says will remain unpublished for a long time to come (case in point to the observation brought forth by his daughters).
titleDescription:
Heritage House Custom Tailors is a culmination of the lifelong experiences of a family business that originated in Bangkok, Thailand in the 1970s, and brings the traditions of a genuinely bespoke and custom tailored product and meshes it seamlessly with not just with the technologies available today to enhance what is delivered, but also tap into the near-future innovations and customize it to augment the overall customer experience.

We are proud to be in the unique position to be able to say that we are one of the very few vertically integrated companies that also owns and operates its own production facility where we custom manufacture each order in-house.  This gives us a near unmatched advantage of ensuring top quality tailoring and workmanship for our customers, along with the capability to be able to grow and scale as newer opportunities unfold.

We selected the knightly Horse to personify our company ethos to highlight that we are a company that is very loyal to both our external and internal customers.  We are focused, intelligent, work with purpose and fully subscribe to the importance of working with chivalry. 

By constantly exploring the boundaries of fabric and design, Heritage House ensures you always look your confident and comfortable best - no matter the occasion.

In 2015, Arch Founded Socialgiver, a boutique lifestyle and travel startup that raises funds for local charities. Socialgiver helps customers make maximum social and environmental impact while they enjoy the best travel and dining experiences with over 500 handpicked brands across Thailand. When you use Socialgiver, at least 50% from your purchases goes to fund amazing projects that are changing lives. To date, Socialgiver has impacted more than half a million lives.

In 2020, Arch founded Wildchain, a mobile tool that brings people together to protect and restore our planet's endangered wildlife and natural zones through digital collectibles (NFTs) on the blockchain that acts as a digital mirror of real-world animal populations. Wildchain enables adoption, awareness, and crowdfunding in a new way that creates real life impact, simply by playing a simple game.

Arch currently sits on the National Board of One Young World, promoting youth participation in social development, and Social Enterprise Thailand, supporting social entrepreneurs across Thailand. He is also a seasoned impact investor with a focus on early-stage innovative ideas that has potential to change the world.

Arch is an Eisenhower Fellow, Global shaper of the World Economic Forum, Edmund Hillary Fellow, UNICEF NextGen Champion, Swedish Institute Fellow, Thailand Institute of Justice Fellow; and has been included in Thailand’s Forbes 30 under 30, Esquire's Men at their Best, Tatler’s GenT, JCI's Outstanding Young Person Award, and The Future List.
titleDescription: Collect adorable animals, grow your very own wildlife sanctuary, and save them from the 6th mass extinction. Wildchain is a climate action tool that lets you adopt wildlife and plant trees to cut your carbon footprint and support real-world conservation efforts - all within a mobile game. Wildchain uses real world data to create in-game environments and experiences that educate and engage users. Players’ actions in the game trigger real-world impact and 100% of our profits go to support wildlife conservation initiatives that are protecting some of the world’s most fragile ecosystems and wildlife species.

titleDescription:
Kata Rocks is a luxury oceanfront development and resort situated on the west coast of Phuket in Thailand.  Having already won 20 international property awards including the title of "Best Apartment in the World", Kata Rocks is already well recognised as being one of the finest developments in Asia.  We have also won the title of "Best Development Marketing in the World" for this project and both of these titles involve beating entries from 110 countries.  More importantly, these were judged by over 50 judges from around the world who are all property professionals.

Kata Rocks consists of 34 Sky Villas all with full sea views and private pools along with all the usual resort facilities such as bar, restaurant, communal pool, spa, gym and wellness centre, lounge, boutique shop and function room. With the development phase coming to an end, the resort is opening at the end of 2013.  Sky Villas are available for purchase or rental.

As the developer, principle owner and MD, I have been responsible for starting Kata Rocks from scratch in 2008.  This initially involved finding investors, purchasing the land, and setting up an office in Phuket.  Over the last 5 years, I have gone on to form an in house sales and marketing team, a finance and legal team, an in house design team, an in house project management team and in house construction management team managing over 800 consultants, contractors, sub-contractors and workers on site.  It was necessary to take over the project management and construction management to ensure that we got the quality product that was required.  With the development phase nearing the finish, we are currently hiring the resort staff and Kata Rocks now directly employs over 80 staff and this will rise to around 140 in early 2014.

titleDescription:
https://vivingrocery.com/

The French know-how adapted to local cuisine

At Vivin, they sells gourmet products from diverse backgrounds, with a focus on the sale of French products and local Thai products. This mixture of culinary culture is how they have made the success of the company.

More than French products, it’s the know-how “made in France” which this young entrepreneur, is using, owing to his knowledge learned from chefs, including Paul Bocuse, whom he worked for. As for Nicolas Vivin, “the quality, the reputation, the image of luxury, originality of products, attention to detail and refinement of taste” are the major assets of the brand.

Becoming the pioneer of “glocal”, the deluxe version

For the future, Nicolas Vivin is looking at it by working on current trends. It needs to be known that Thailand opens to the wine market and that local consumers are looking for products that blend well with the wine. For this reason, VIVIN gourmet grocery is looking to support local consumption habits.

The second trend is also before the implementation of local products to bear witness to the integration of local eating habits. For Nicolas Vivin, the future is in the “glocal”. It is a compromise between marketing said “global” and marketing said “local”.

This is to develop a global strategy while ensuring create adaptations to local conditions. “The interest is to be a leader or pioneer with luxury profile. – “I think it is logical to integrate into the local economy and work with those around us. Buchette made from local goat milk, Saucisson made from local pork with cashew nuts from Phuket, Larb dried duck magret “Larb Phet”, (spicy Northeast duck prosciutto), and Foie gras with dried mango is one of the best examples of this mixture of flavors.”

Nicolas Vivin would also continue its expansion in Thailand with the motto: “the courage to adapt what we think to be perfect.”

Excerpts taken from: http://www.franceagroalimentaire.com/

title: Founder
summary:
In a world that often rushes past pain and overlooks invisible struggles, I choose to slow down—and listen.
.
My name is Milla Budiarto, and I’m a mental health advocate based in Phuket, Thailand, committed to creating compassionate, connected communities where people feel seen, safe, and supported. I’m passionate about creating change where it matters most: in the minds, hearts, and lives of people.
For me, advocacy isn't just a role—it’s a way of living. With a deep-rooted belief that mental health is not just personal—it's communal—I’ve dedicated my work to fostering environments that promote awareness, healing, and resilience.
.
Whether supporting individuals in emotional distress, organizing community events to raise mental health awareness, or delivering speaking engagements, I lead with one core value: empathy. True empathy—the kind that sits with someone in silence, walks beside them without judgment, and believes in their capacity to heal.
.
I work across cultures and causes to bridge gaps in understanding and bring mental wellness into everyday conversation. Through local workshops, school programs, community events, and quiet one-on-one conversations, I strive to break the stigma around mental health and open up meaningful dialogues in spaces where silence used to dominate. My work is deeply rooted in trauma-informed care, emotional literacy, and community empowerment. 
.
What I value:
•	Authentic human connection
•	Mental health accessibility and equity
•	Building sustainable, empowered communities
•	Turning vulnerability into strength
.
What I advocate for:
•	Accessible, stigma-free mental health support
•	Empathy as a daily practice, not just a feeling
•	Safe spaces for children and adults to express, heal, and grow
•	Resilient, more compassionate communities from the inside out
.
Healing doesn’t happen in isolation. It happens when we feel safe enough to show up as we are—in community, in stillness, and in courage.
.
If you’re working in mental health, education, community development—or simply believe in the power of empathy—I’d love to connect. I'm always open to collaborations, conversations, and creative ideas. Let’s collaborate to make mental wellness not just a goal, but a shared reality in our communities.
.
#MentalHealthPhuket #MentalHealthMatters #EmpathyInAction #CompassionateLeadership #CommunityWellbeing

titleDescription:
Phuket FantaSea, winner of Thailand’s “Best Attraction Award”, is a unique cultural theme park that promises to be the ultimate in night-time entertainment on the holiday resort island of Phuket. Inspired by Thailand’s rich and exotic heritage, Phuket FantaSea not only showcases the charm and beauty of Thailand, but also enriches ancient Thai traditions with the wonder of cutting edge technology and special effects. The result is a stunning 30-acre theme complex, packed with a multitude of activities and entertainment:

Festival Village is truly a shopper’s paradise. Here visitors can buy a wide range of high quality souvenir products from all corners of Thailand and enjoy all-night-long festivities, parades, craft demonstrations, street shows, live bands, elephant rides, games and much more!

The Golden Kinnaree Buffet Restaurant is one of the world’s grandest buffet restaurants that has to be seen to be believed. Amidst the mystical Himmaphan forest setting, this 4,000 seat dining facility offers visitors a sumptuous feast of Royal Thai dishes and international delicacies, prepared by some of Thailand’s finest chefs.

Fantasy of a Kingdom is the award-winning Las Vegas style production spectacle colorfully blending the beauty of Thai culture with magical illusions, 4-D effects, aerial ballet, acrobatics, pyrotechnics, special effects, exciting stunts and a grand finale featuring over 30 elephants, all into one unforgettably unique theatrical experience guaranteed to thrill and delight visitors of all ages and nationalities. (Show time is 21:00; Reservation is highly recommended; “Theater Gold seats”, with the best view of show, are available at extra charge)

Created at cost of over 3,500 million Baht, Phuket FantaSea adds a whole new dimension to theme park entertainment, with marvelous attractions not found anywhere else in the world.
name: Phuket FantaSea

“Coaching”… what a bunch of unregulated woo-woo BS. 

At least that’s what I thought.

Until I let my “anti-woo” guard down and made a concerted effort to set down my biases.

It took getting an ADHD diagnosis, months on the meds, a stack of self-directed research to realise I’ll never be “neurotypical”.

I still felt “lacking” as a human. 

A fraud as a professional.

A flakey friend and partner.

In my search for an ADHD therapist I discovered an alternative. A coach that didn’t speak in esoteric platitudes. 

Felt relatable from our very first conversation. 

Has lived experience of neurodivergence and a special interest in ADHD.

Best of all, has actual credentials, recognised internationally.

The impact has been immense. And shockingly measurable.

• Increased confidence in my decision-making.

• Better able to distinguish fact from fiction when the inner critic emerges

• Improvements in boundary-setting

And most surprisingly?

A clearer picture of my future, the steps to get there, and what “success” means to me

……………………………


Hi, I’m Helen. 

A recovering tech marketer and copywriter. 

Now shifting from consulting and mentoring towards a blend that includes “pure coaching” options.

I have just 2 spots available for 1:1 coaching in December.

Rates start from £45 per 60 minute session.

Why so cheap? 

This rate won’t be available forever.

Things are weird. Cash is tight for a lot of people. But it’s those people who need support the most.

Curious but not sure if coaching is right for you?

DM me for a no strings chat and I’ll give you alllll the details.

titleDescription:
Ready to design your career and life around what YOU want?

I’m here to support your mission.

For 10+ years I’ve been a small business owner, consultant, and freelancer.

I’ve spent hundreds of hours mentoring founders, marketers and consultants (including through GrowthMentor). 

Before that, I managed teams across Europe in adtech while juggling budgets for the world’s biggest online ad spenders.

Sounds impressive, when put like that, eh?

But what if I told you that in all that time, I never felt enough. 

Constantly stressed, anxious, prone to depression and burnout. 

Always expecting the worst.

Waiting to be exposed as an imposter. Poised to be fired.

Then, life events compounded all those masked challenges.

I hit breaking point.

Next, aged 44, I was diagnosed with ADHD. 

Suddenly the struggle made sense.

The patterns I thought were personal flaws were just my brain working differently to “the norm”. 

The coping mechanisms, while not all healthy, were life rafts where ideal systems and strategies should have been.

That’s when coaching changed everything for me. And why I’m making a Big Change.

Now, I’m enrolled in a coaching diploma program.

I’ve seen too many people crushing themselves to fit a template made for someone else. 

I’m here to support anyone ready to tackle exactly that.

What I bring:

- 10+ years of real entrepreneurial experience: the pivots, the burnout, the recovery

- Background leading teams and mentoring founders through practical challenges

- Lived experience with late-diagnosed ADHD

- A no-woo approach to helping you identify and amplify your hidden strengths 

TAKING BOOKINGS FOR LATE NOVEMBER NOW

Limited Early Bird Spots: 1:1 Coaching

Lock in up to 6 private sessions at £49/hour 

DM for an honest chat about coaching.


NOTE: All pre booked sessions start after 27th November. Terms and conditions apply and are made crystal clear before any booking.

title: Founder & CEO
summary:
Bryan Lunt is a trailblazing visionary in the Hospitality & Wellness industry, renowned for his innovative approach to blending luxury, natural healing, and transformative experiences. As the Founder of The Beach Samui—a multi-award-winning boutique hotel and plant medicine-enhanced sanctuary on the idyllic island of Koh Samui—Bryan has redefined the concept of holistic hospitality.

With an illustrious career spanning over two decades, Bryan previously served as Chairman & CEO of one of Asia's largest privately-held shared-ownership hospitality brands. During his tenure, he played a pivotal role in shaping the company's growth and success, solidifying his reputation as a leader in the shared-ownership and club membership space.

In 2016, Bryan's passion for wellbeing led to the creation of 180º Wellness, a groundbreaking series of retreat-style programs in Phuket, Thailand, designed to nurture the mind, body, and soul through fitness, mindfulness, and holistic practices. These programs became a testament to his commitment to empowering individuals on their wellness journeys.

The post-Covid era marked a profound evolution in Bryan's personal and professional focus. Driven by a deep belief in the power of natural healing, he embraced plant-based therapies, nootropics, and cannabinoids as integral components of whole-body health. This inspired the birth of SABAI SABAI™️, a decentralized wellness movement set to launch in 2024 at The Beach Samui. SABAI SABAI™️ represents Bryan's bold vision to democratize wellness, combining cutting-edge science, ancient wisdom, and community-driven innovation.

Guided by a dedicated and visionary team, Bryan is pioneering a transformative approach to hospitality that seamlessly integrates natural wellness, healing, and exclusive club membership programs. His mission is to bridge the digital and physical realms, creating immersive experiences that redefine wellbeing. With an eye toward the future, Bryan is charting a course toward an exhilarating NFT-driven metaverse, where community, ownership, and holistic wellness converge. His leadership continues to inspire, shaping the future of wellness and hospitality across Asia and beyond.

title: Founder and Challenge Hiker
summary:
Hi there. I’m Jason, an early-stage investor, strategist, and community builder focused on Emerging Asia’s consumer markets.

Facing a second cancer diagnosis in April 2024 and living with the disease forced a reset. It made me slow down and get deliberate about where my time and energy go.

This has narrowed my focus to two things. The first is conquering The Lycian Way Challenge, a 540-kilometer endurance hike I created in Türkiye this spring to support Movember, men’s health, and low-cost rural cancer screening.

The second is continuing to build our media-led consumer investments at GDV.

Two goals. One intentional path forward.

———

Over the last 20+ years, I’ve worked across technology, consumer, and global brands, including YouTube, Tencent, Lazada, and organizations such as The PGA of America, Vail Resorts, Samsung, Audi, and Türk Telekom.

That work has taken me across both developed and emerging markets, spanning media, technology, and consumer businesses. It has included work alongside startups, NGOs, multicultural teams, and public sector partners. Along the way, I’ve contributed to teams that raised more than $40 million, including work tied to ASEAN and G-20 initiatives.

Relocating to Southeast Asia in 2019 sharpened the work. Living in Thailand and Vietnam has meant building close relationships with founders, investors, educators, and policymakers, and staying close to the ground.

I believe strongly in grassroots entrepreneurship. Partnering with people in rural and under-resourced communities has been some of the most rewarding work of my career. I’ve seen how a little planning, encouragement, and small money can turn a skill into a livelihood or a tiny market stall into a thriving business. Coming back later to see these entrepreneurs succeed is something I never take for granted.

Outside of work, I support causes including cancer survivorship, hunger and homelessness, the environment, and historic preservation. I enjoy skiing, kayaking, camping, brass bands, and reading everything I can get my hands on. Having traveled to and spent time in 58 countries, I find endless inspiration in people and places.

If I can help in any way, please reach out. A quick, thoughtful note is always appreciated and will get the attention it deserves. No unsolicited sales pitches or coaching offers, please.

Lastly, all opinions expressed here on LinkedIn are my own.
titleDescription:
The Lycian Way Challenge is a 540-kilometer thru-hike tracing Türkiye’s Mediterranean coastline along ancient footpaths and trading routes. In Spring 2026, I’ll set out to complete the Lycian Way in support of Movember, men’s health, and expanded access to cancer screening.

After facing cancer twice, including a second diagnosis in April 2024, I’m taking on this cross-country hike as part of one of Movember’s largest global challenges this year.

I’ll share the journey from the trail through storytelling and on-location cinematography, alongside partners, sponsors, and donors committed to advancing conversations around men’s health and early detection.

Learn more and help power the hike at lycianwaychallenge.com.

title: Strategic Advisor
summary:
Hospitality isn’t a product.

It’s a human experience.

I believe the future of hospitality belongs to owners and operators who refuse to choose between performance and soul. Those who understand that identity, culture, and presence are not “soft” ideas, but strategic advantages.

I’m an owner-operator and hospitality entrepreneur based in Bangkok, currently serving as Managing Director of Lohas Residences Sukhumvit and CXO at Guestasy. Over the past decade, I’ve turned bold ideas into working realities: from transforming a 144-key independent boutique hotel into one of Bangkok’s top quarantine properties, to co-creating SparQ Spaces, the city’s first media-integrated hotel studio.

I hold a Master’s in Strategic Marketing from Imperial College and a Hospitality Certification from Cornell, but my real education has come from navigating the tension between legacy and reinvention inside family-run businesses: where decisions are personal, long-term, and deeply human.

In 2023, I co-founded Zen Den Coffee House and expanded SparQ Spaces under the concept “Stay. Relax. Shoot.” Exploring how media, creativity, and place intersect through the lens of media tourism.

Today, I’m the founder of Stay Unbranded, a philosophy and advisory platform for next-gen hospitality owners who crave freedom from templates, scale-at-all-costs thinking, and soulless brand playbooks.

Stay Unbranded stands for: 

• Identity before scale

• Experience before efficiency

• People before process

If you’re building, investing in, or rethinking hospitality at the edge of culture, technology, and conscious travel, we’ll likely have a lot to talk about.

I believe work should feel like play and if we’re lucky, even like art.

titleDescription:
Montagé is the Andaman Gourmet salt brand aiming to inspire and to introduce new flavors to the culinary world of salts.

Our journey began a few years back where we set out to travel the world, leaving our respective homes and families in Europe and North America. We first moved to the Kingdom of Thailand and it was here, where we experienced the modern side of Asia, great casual fun times with friends, and deep cultural roots and beliefs. We traveled Thailand, the Andaman and across the region, only to discover that each of the individual places we visited offered new and exciting aromas and flavors.

We enjoy the natural elements and simplicity that quality gourmet food can deliver, removing all non-natural additives, to taste food the way it was always meant to be. At the center of this world of flavor is salt, a natural mineral we consume every day. It can come with different textures, colors and tastes and it belongs to every one’s household, in every kitchen of this world. This brings us to Thailand, the Andaman Sea, and its surrounding region, which provides for a truly unique and extraordinary experience, where natural aromas and flavors, delicately extracted from locally found plants and minerals, come together in harmony. 

With Montagé, we aim to bring these outstanding aroma and flavor experiences to each and every meal, using only natural and clean ingredients with our gourmet salts, harnessing the natural essence of each component and delivering it to your home. 

Let us take you on a journey!

Most local businesses are playing Russian Roulette with their revenue.
On Saturday, your venue is full. By Wednesday, it echoes with emptiness.

I’m Adam, Founder of Review to Revenue. We help restaurants, cafés, and retail venues stop gambling on foot traffic and start automating it.

title: Scent Marketing, Aroma Branding, Ambient Scenting, Scent Design
summary:
Neon Group introduced the concept of scent branding to Thailand and Asia via the NeoScent brand and it has proven to be a huge success.

What is Scent Marketing?
For many years, various types of businesses have been utilizing the science of smell and Scent Marketing to their advantage, allowing them to reinforce brand loyalty and generate higher profits. Companies like Nike, Jimmy Choo, Abercrombie & Fitch and Harrods benefit from this by dispersing specific, strategically formulated scents throughout their stores, and in some cases, even in their products. These scents are used to trigger a certain emotion in potential customers, subtly encouraging them to not only attach a scent to their brand (brand image), but also to spend more time in their stores or places of business (customer experience), all the while creating positive memories with those scents that will keep them going back to the product or service (brand loyalty).

Why Scent Marketing Works
Scent Marketing, with its more subtle approach, is very unassuming and works on a subconscious level, giving you an invisible key to the “driver’s seat”. Scent is the strongest of the five senses tied to memory and emotion. This gives scent marketing the perfect opportunity to attract customers by playing an essential role in transforming perceptions and creating an experience for maximum effect. It helps to explore more opportunities for business and helps to further push the essence of a company’s brand.

See more at www.neoscent.com

No theatrics. No vague wellness language. Just methods that reliably reduce physiological noise and improve decision‑making, resilience, and presence.

I work with executives, founders, creatives, hospitality leaders, and high‑performing professionals navigating pressure, transition, or burnout. I also collaborate alongside medical and allied holistic professionals, supporting nervous system regulation and functional resilience without diagnosing or treating disease.

At its core, my work helps people feel alive, clear, and engaged again—not by pushing harder, but by rebuilding internal capacity from the inside out.

If you’re responsible for the performance and well‑being of a team—or you’re a high performer running on empty—I provide grounded, systems‑based support that creates real change, not temporary relief.

titleDescription:
About the Show:
🔥 "Unfiltered Humankind" is a sanctuary for raw, unscripted human stories. We strip away the highlight reels and curated personas to explore the messy, beautiful, and often unspoken truths of what it means to be human. No rehearsed answers. No performative vulnerability. Just real voices, real struggles, and real resilience.

We’re Seeking:
People with stories that challenge, heal, or ignite change. Whether you’ve:

- Survived the unimaginable
- Failed spectacularly (and learned)
- Defied societal norms
- Found light in darkness
- Or simply embraced your "flaws"

    ...we want to hear your truth. No story is too "niche," too painful, or too unconventional.

Why Share With Us?

    ✨ Impact: Your story could be the lifeline someone needs.
    🌍 Authentic Platform: We prioritize depth over virality.
    🤝 Respectful Space: Your boundaries, triggers, and voice are sacred here.
    🎙️ Professional Production: High-quality audio/video to honor your narrative.

Ideal Guests Are:

    Courageous truth-tellers (not "influencers").
    Willing to explore nuance – no binary "hero/villain" tropes.
    Comfortable with depth over soundbites.

The environment needs our help: let’s take our responsible steps for our children and grandchildren and the many generations to come.

title: Private Investor
summary:
Nick is an investor and entrepreneur, with over 35 years’ experience. He continues to retain active management roles at a number of ventures that he founded, or co-founded, across an array of industries that include management consulting, digital marketing, real-estate development & property management, catering, and alternative energy.
 
Nick received a B.Sc. from University of Bristol (UK) and MBA from Sasin School of Management at Chulalongkorn University (Thailand). In 2019, Nick was a recipient of Sasin's inaugural Distinguished Alumni Award.

At Sasin School of Management, Nick served as a faculty member, having been an advisor and mentor in new venture formulation since 2006, and, since 2010, a lecturer in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR), Sustainability Management, Business Ethics, and Entrepreneurship. In 2011, Nick founded and was the Director of the Sasin Center for Sustainability Management. And, in 2014, he co-founded the Sasin Entrepreneurship Center, serving as its Faculty Advisor. The two centers merged under Nick’s leadership in 2018 to become the Sustainability and Entrepreneurship Center @ Sasin, where Nick served as its Founding Director. In 2019, Nick was invited to join Sasin’s management team as the Deputy Director for Strategy, Innovation, and Impact, a position he maintained until his early retirement from the School at the end of 2023.

Nick’s involvement with non-profit organizations included roles as a Director of the Inventors Association of Thailand, President of the Bangkok Professional Chapter of Net Impact, and Thailand’s representative of the Caux Round Table. In 2010, he cofounded and served as the President of the Corporate Responsibility & Ethics Association for Thai Enterprise (CREATE) until 2025. Nick also served on the Screening Committee of the Bangkok Venture Club, Thailand’s largest angel investment network, since its foundation in 2014. 

Primarily through his non-profit roles, Nick was honoured by the World Economic Forum (WEF) as a Young Global Leader in 2011. And, in 2012, he established the Bangkok Hub of the WEF’s Global Shapers community that comprises highly exceptional individuals under the age of 30. 

Nick is an active member of the Rotary Club of Bangkok South, Thailand’s largest such service club, and was its President in 2012/13.

title: INDEPENDENT FILM
summary:
A self-taught and pro-active film-maker, Kaprice’s formative years were spent in London.  In 1993 (pre-internet), Kaprice founded and hosted Peeping Tom's", in the heart of Soho.  The first club of it's kind, Kaprice provided a free information and networking resource, to serve a 'new wave' of emerging film professionals for 7 years.  

During this period, as a screenwriter & director, and rapidly following on from his acclaimed short, “Honeymoon Beyond”, Kaprice made his 35mm feature film debut with "the hurting".  
 
Then, in January 2000, he chose a different life, re-locating to Thailand, to set-up his second feature film, "Butterfly Man”. For a brief spell, Kaprice funded himself by directing TV Commercials.   

In 2001, once Butterfly Man had wrapped, he decided to apply his directing skills, and experience in international film, to provide weekly screen acting workshops.  He founded fluid (registered as fluid film co ltd), with a mission to cultivate a pool of quality actors.  Within months, mission completed, fluid had secured an exclusive reputation for representing screen actors, that met the expectations and demand of foreign producers coming to shoot in Thailand.  

Until the pandemic, Kaprice’s prepping, repping and strategising, helped enable Bangkok-based actors to land speaking roles on profile movies, and the key building blocks to their careers. New generations of aspiring actors continue to attend his screen acting workshops that still run today.  

Throughout those years, Kaprice has kept busy, directing two more feature films, cast twelve international productions, and received a variety of credits for international feature film, that include: producer, editor, assistant director, screenwriter, script consultant, dialogue editor, dialogue coach, acting coach, and as an actor!

title: Founder & Coach
summary:
I build mental performance tools for people in hard moments — the kind where your plan fell apart, your tech is useless, and the only thing left is what you trained your mind to do.

That work spans 20 years across six continents — coaching athletes through ultramarathons, working in mental health care, guiding life coaching clients through major transitions, and speaking to rooms from Basel to Bangkok. The thread connecting all of it: the tools that work best are the ones that don't depend on anything external.

Right now I'm deep in the work of turning what I've learned into courses, frameworks, and free resources — and writing a biweekly newsletter called Dispatches where I share the tools, the thinking behind them, and honest notes on what AI is changing for coaches and creators.

I live in Koh Samui, Thailand, with my wife Anja, where we run a street dog rescue and I write from a kitchen table with two dogs under my feet.

Start here: hillseeker.com/dispatches

title: Managing Director
summary:
Senior executive and board director with 25+ years in energy, water and infrastructure, leading P&Ls and supply chains across Asia and the Middle East. My work sits at the intersection of sustainability, strategy and operations, using business and infrastructure to benefit communities while advancing decarbonisation and water stewardship.

Raised in Bangkok and shaped by 11 years studying in the UK and US, I have worked across Asia and the Middle East and led regional teams across cultures. These experiences reinforced my belief that inclusive, diverse environments lead to better decisions and more resilient organisations.

Over 26 years at Shell, including as Regional GM for the bitumen / Construction & Road business in Asia & the Middle East, I was accountable for P&L, supply chain and safety across multiple countries, enabling infrastructure that helps children get to school and patients reach hospital care. That connection between operational excellence and real-world impact has shaped who I am as a leader.

Wanting to put sustainability and societal impact at the centre of my work, I left Shell to create a different kind of platform. I founded Green Frog Innovation to help accelerate Thailand’s sustainability journey and partnered with Asuene, a leading Japanese climate tech company, to support industry in carbon accounting and emissions reduction. That chapter deepened my understanding of how data, technology and collaboration can scale impact.

Today, as Managing Director of Ecolab Ltd. (Thailand) and Head of Nalco Water Manufacturing for Thailand & Vietnam, I apply that purpose at scale – providing country leadership and P&L accountability while helping customers across hospitality, healthcare, manufacturing and food & beverage use water, energy and resources more efficiently and responsibly. I hold certifications in carbon footprinting and life cycle assessment to ensure my ESG work is grounded in practice, not theory.

Outside work, I care about education. Since 2020 I have served on the foundation board of NIST International School, a not-for-profit school in Bangkok, contributing to governance, strategy and long-term sustainability. I try to live by a yin–yang mindset: no one is perfect, and every strength has a shadow, so I focus on developing my strengths, addressing my blind spots and building complementary teams that challenge constructively and give others room to lead. I enjoy connecting and learning from people who share an interest in sustainability, infrastructure and societal impact to exchange ideas and scale positive change.

summary:
Asst. Prof. Dr. Chution Savini was the wildlife scientist for Wildlife Conservation Society (WCS) for over 10 years. Her interest is conducting the wildlife ecology study and create conservation strategies using a science-based approach to strengthen wildlife conservation and research in Thailand. She has been a full-time lecturer at the International College for Sustainability Studies (SWUIC), Srinakharinwirot University (Thailand). With SWUIC, she served as Assistant Dean for Student Affairs (2012-2015) and Assistant Dean for Research (2015-2016). Besides her academic work she participated in several wildlife conservations projects at both national and international levels. She was the Chair of The Association for Tropical Biology and Conservation (2013-2015). Its role is to promote scientific understanding and conservation of tropical environments by organizing international conferences and publishing scientific journals. The various case studies I encountered helped me with the development of class materials. 

Besides her academic work she participated in several wildlife conservations projects at both national and international levels. She was the Chair of The Association for Tropical Biology and Conservation (2013-2015). Its role is to promote scientific understanding and conservation of tropical environments by organizing international conferences and publishing scientific journals. The various case studies  encountered helped her with the development of class materials. She served as a committee member in the graduate level Conservation Programs. Dr. Chution is currently involved actively in resolving human-elephant conflicts (HEC) in communities surrounding the park and conducting various tools to gain the engagement of local communities and park rangers to identify the HEC mitigation measures. title: Company Owner
summary:
I am a chef with a passion for holistic, sustainable, and innovative food experiences. Over the past two decades, I’ve honed my craft through Michelin-star training with culinary legends such as Michael Caines, Gordon Ramsay, Thomas Keller, and Corey Lee. My journey in the culinary world has been rich and varied, taking me from various roles at prestigious establishments like Gidleigh Park Hotel and The French Laundry to guest culinary tutor at Leiths Cookery School in London.

In 2015, after experiencing burnout, I embarked on a transformative culinary inspiration tour to Northern Thailand, which led to a pivotal shift in my cooking philosophy - from classical to holistic. This new approach focuses on wellness, sustainability, and plant-based cuisine, which has become the cornerstone of my current work.

Currently, I work as a Wellness Chef Consultant and Private Chef, working on diverse projects across Spain, France, Taiwan, the Maldives, and Thailand. My consultancy business operates from our head office in Cheltenham, UK, with additional offices in Phuket, Thailand, and now in Galway, Ireland. 

Our Phuket office, nestled in the jungle, serves as a creative hub for our recipe development and marketing efforts, particularly for our online recipe blog www.holisticchefacademy.com. Here, we also launched 'Life On Plants,' Thailand’s first plant-based zero-waste kitchen, during the pandemic lockdown, pioneering healthy vegan meal delivery on the Island of Phuket.

In addition to my consultancy work, I also served as the Director of Culinary Development at Thanyapura Health & Sports Resort in Phuket for three years, where I led initiatives to promote health-conscious dining experiences for professional athletes. 

Looking ahead, I am thrilled to announce our latest venture: an eco-glamping, permaculture farm, and culinary studio in County Galway, West Ireland. Set to open in 2028, this project embodies my commitment to sustainable living and culinary innovation. With this new business, we aim to create a space where guests can reconnect with nature, enjoy organic, farm-to-table cuisine, and immerse themselves in holistic wellness practices.

My journey continues to evolve, driven by a desire to inspire and educate others about the power of food as a tool for personal development, sustainability, and community building.

✨ 𝐈 𝐠𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐓𝐑𝐀𝐍𝐒𝐈𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍, 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐝𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭.✨

There comes a moment in life when the familiar no longer fits. You feel stuck—whether in your job, your organization, a relationship, or even your own mind. You know something needs to change, but uncertainty holds you back. Stress, frustration, and self-doubt dim your energy, leaving you searching for clarity, direction, and purpose.  

I guide individuals and organizations through these TRANSITIONS, personal and professional ones, helping them navigate change with confidence, step out of the comfort zone, and move into a space of joy, alignment, learning, and growth.  

🔥For 𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗶𝘃𝗶𝗱𝘂𝗮𝗹𝘀 : I support personal and professional transformation through deep coaching, mentoring, and experiential training in behavioral skills. Whether you're feeling lost, overwhelmed, or disconnected, I help you reconnect with yourself and move forward with clarity.  

🔥For 𝗼𝗿𝗴𝗮𝗻𝗶𝘇𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 : Just as people evolve, so must businesses. I specialize in 𝙘𝙪𝙡𝙩𝙪𝙧𝙖𝙡 𝙩𝙧𝙖𝙣𝙨𝙞𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙨, guiding companies to move from a Training culture to a Learning culture, and from a non-client-centric approach to a truly customer-focused mindset. A thriving organization is one that embraces continuous growth and puts people—both employees and customers—at the center of its evolution.  

𝐇𝐎𝐖 𝐈 𝐇𝐄𝐋𝐏

💡𝘾𝙤𝙖𝙘𝙝𝙞𝙣𝙜 & 𝙈𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙤𝙧𝙞𝙣𝙜 : deep, holistic support tailored to personal and professional transformation.
  
💡𝙏𝙧𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙞𝙣𝙜 & 𝙁𝙖𝙘𝙞𝙡𝙞𝙩𝙖𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣 : practical, experiential learning that builds behavioral and emotional intelligence and skills.  

💡𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙨𝙪𝙡𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 : people-centered solutions to shape learning-driven, customer-centric cultures.

Whether you’re an individual facing a challenging transition or an organization ready for cultural transformation, I help you step into the light of new possibilities.

titleDescription:
WHY
After 27 years of supporting human well-being and development, I've added the deepest dimension of my work: Sacred Reconnection for Women.

I support accomplished women reclaiming what went dormant while succeeding in a man's world: their self-alignment, natural embodiment, and inner flow.

I believe the world shifts when women integrate the part they learned to suppress in order to lead, perform, and achieve.

This part knows how to flow, feel, surrender, and simply BE. 
She is their Shakti, their Sacred Feminine.


HOW
Each 1:1 in-person session is individually tailored, guided by integrity and deep respect for every woman's unique rhythm and truth.
Through sacred witnessing, somatic work, and conscious masculine presence, I help women transition from constant doing to intentional being, from external achievement to inner alignment, so they can experience deeper embodiment, authenticity, and flow.

As a man facilitating this process, I don't teach women how to embody their Feminine, they already know. My role is to create a grounded, safe, masculine container where their Shakti can unfold freely, held in full trust and reverence.

This is POLARITY work. 
Wholeness emerges through the integration of our inner Shiva (consciousness, structure, doing) and Shakti (energy, flow, being).


FOR WHO
Women who are self-aware and devoted to their own growth. 
Women ready for sacred inner work.


FROM WHO
This work calls upon everything I've lived, learned, and become over 26 years of coaching, facilitation, and human development, as well as 49 years of being human. It touches every part of me: my strengths and wounds, my knowledge and skills, my journey and life purpose.

Facilitating this reconnection is both the most demanding and the most fulfilling work I've ever done.
It's not just what I do. 
It's who I am.


NEXT STEP
If something in you resonates, honor that knowing.
Reach out for a simple conversation to get more details or share with a woman who needs it.

title: Director
summary:
Mr. Autthanop Pandkamnerd, the Chief Executive Officer of CISSA Group Co., Ltd and its affiliated companies, boasts two decades of expertise in construction engineering and real estate development. He holds a Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering from Prince of Songkla University and a Master's in Business Management from Ramkhamhaeng University.

In 2005, Mr. Autthanop established his company, CISSA Group, in Southern Thailand. His inaugural project, The Club & Spa, a luxury pool villa residential development in Phuket province, set the stage for subsequent ventures. Over the years, he successfully launched several residential projects, including Vanilla House, The Eva, Eva Beach, and the renowned Coco Group developments: Coco Rawai, Coco Kamala, and The 8 Pool Villa.

In 2018, Mr. Autthanop shifted his business model towards Investment Property (IP) and collaborated with Wyndham Asia & Pacific Group on the Wyndham Grand Nai Harn Beach Phuket, a 5-star premium hotel with 353 luxurious condotel and hotel rooms. In 2020, he acquired Natai Beach Resort in Pang-Nga, to transform it into a world-class well-being centre and resort, scheduled for completion in 2026.

Beyond his professional endeavours, Mr. Autthanop is deeply committed to society and philanthropy. He founded the Andaman Natural Resources Conservation Foundation and the Bird of Hope Foundation, both dedicated to supporting children battling cancer. Additionally, he serves as the Honorary Consul of the Republic of Kazakhstan in Phuket, receiving a letter of appreciation from the Deputy Prime Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Kazakhstan for his assistance to Kazakhstani citizens during the Covid-19 pandemic.

Mr. Autthanop continues to pursue his IP roadmap, with numerous new projects in the pipeline. His remarkable expertise in development and IP ventures has earned him a stellar reputation among investors in Thailand and abroad.

titleDescription:
Koh Lipe Thailand (also written as ‘Ko Lipe’) is a small Thai island with beautiful beaches surrounded by pristine coral reefs. Koh Lipe is part of the Tarutao National Marine park and is next to a group of uninhabited islands with deserted beaches, waterfalls and breathtaking viewpoints to visit. If you are looking for a place to have a relaxed beach holiday on a beautiful island in Thailand, look no further than Koh Lipe.

At Castaway Resort the spirit of ‘The Beach’ is brought to life. Amid the beautiful setting nature has given Koh Lipe, our spacious wooden bungalows and large chill out deck overlooking the beach definitely make Castaway the most atmospheric of Koh Lipe’s resorts.

Stay in one of our spacious bungalows and re-connect with a simpler life. Made from hardwood's and natural materials the design of our bungalows draws heavily on traditional Thai architecture and building techniques. Designed to capture the winds, cooling breezes are funnelled under the eaves into the bedroom keeping the temperature inside comfortable all year long.

In the evenings under the stars and with the sound of the waves for company sit on the deck and share a memorable evening with friends and family whilst enjoying a cold beer or one of our signature cocktails.

title: Managing Director
summary:
Tipaphat Seesathorn (Mia), Founder and Managing Director of Yimtee, is a young driven and passionate entrepreneur that has always been health conscious and caring about the well being of the people around her. Her professional background shows high achievements in creating corporate and promotional events.

Being born in Nakhonsawan province, Central Thailand. Mia is of Chinese decent with a strong work ethic and strives for success whilst never giving up on any challenge that comes her way. Mia completed her bachelor degree at Kasetsart University Bangkok in Humanity and a major in English. She realized that she has a passion in languages and has completed a 3 month course in German,1 month course in intermediate French, and will soon take her Chinese and Japanese language skills to the next level. 

Mia has a strong level of commitment to social responsibility. She has an attention for detail in completing her projects to the best excellence. She takes initiative with regards to Marketing actions and strategy with positive mindset.

Besides having a passion for cultures and languages, she also has a strong enthusiasm for music and traveling. Having already been to various foreign countries, she has gathered quite a unique selection of music genres and brought it to another level as shown on her Soundcloud platform. https://soundcloud.com/moonfaceth/tracks

Her vision is to build a life where she doesn’t need to take a vacation from. 

== Artist Bio == 

Having been part of the house and techno music industry since a very early stage, it was only a matter of time until Moonface tried her hand at DJ-ing. Next thing you know she is casting spells and making the booty shake. A resident behind the decks of Baba Beach Club (Phuket), Moonface is quickly climbing up the ladder of Thailand night life scene. After a stellar performance at Kolour’s 9th Anniversary, Moonface has gathered quite a following with her tribal influenced booty-shaking tunes, sassy disco, energetic electro/ techno, breakbeats, tech house and Afro house that has captured many hearts. Just as the moon shines bright, Moonface’s presence behind the decks shines bright as well, if not, brighter.
name: Tipaphat Seesathorn
